reviews.review_description:
The graphics are decent, the storyline is good, the mechanics are really good, having teammates is cool, the hord/ wave of zombies at the end of each level, is awesome, the strategic aspect of having miscellaneous weapons that you can set up (by utilizing keys found throughout the levels) to help and your team is great, how you can mix and match different setups to for your playing style, the weapons are great, there's lots of different zombie types... I could go on, but I won't.

Really good game, really happy that I got it on sale for $7, but it's worth $20-$30.

The gameplay itself is really fun, especially when dealing with the larger crowds of zeke. My issue comes down to how the a.i. teammates function. They often stay put and don’t react to being hurt by toxic gas or when dealing with large crowds of zeke during final wave events. 

It’d be nice to one day have multiplayer or customizable skins.
Hoping we get more weapons as well!

Overall 9/10!

reviews.review_title: Good game but overpriced and flawed
reviews.review_description:
It’s absolutely a great game because of weapon, munition and grenade crates, ridiculous amount of zombies (750-3000 zombies during standoffs against swarms and hordes or 30-100 zombies during regular combat),guns and teammates, starter weapons and more but here is the downside

It’s bugged and the ai teammates are either standing somewhere and doing nothing or just follow you when they are not supposed to, they also don’t react to damage and only react to damage when they are at very low health they decide to heal themselves so other than them healing themselves, they rely on YOU to heal them and they sometimes don’t even stand still when you are healing teammates.

It’s not like it’s THAT bad, it’s just not the best, the story and graphics of this game fall behind the walking dead saints and sinners chapter 1 and 2, the advantages this game has is the amount of zombies

Unlike the walking dead saints and sinners games, this game has way more zombies than just 5-10 zombies each map, like I said earlier, there are 750-3000 zombies during hordes or 30-100 zombies during regular combat, great for people who want to gun down overwhelming forces of zombies under pressure and also recommended to strategic people

I really like this game and I'm not one of those that want to go through a game more than one time but this! I started on easy as I'm not very good at shooter games and when I was through I did it on medium and now I'm on hard, and wow it's hard now.
I love the hoards.

Kill a few zombies, kill lots of zombies, kill a few zombies, kill lots of zombies and so it goes. It gets old. The scenes change thankfully, but that’s the game. It lacks content, has a minimal story, and you can’t interact with most things in the environment. Breaking open cases is most of the interaction and your hand and gun go thru the lock repeatedly rather than hitting it. I find this to be cheap. Shouldn’t cost more than $10.

This seems like a cheap lazy cash grab of a game. Weapons work well but thats about the only positive thing I have to say. I could count the number of different zombie models on a single hand and look cheap, and zombies can hit you from like five feet in front of you. The missions also lack creativity, the first mission is literally going to rescue survivors by turning on the electricity, then you literally abandon them after you’ve fought the horde and do the same exact thing in a planetarium instead of a dance floor. Ai teammates suck, they cant heal, they can’t use grenades, they cant find stuff, they just go from point a to point b and stand there, they barely ever move unless the player gets too far, even if they are getting attacked, they stand there. There is just nothing great here, most objects aren’t intractable, zombie bodies disappear, special zombies glow red and makes them look hideous and out of place, it is incredibly repetitive and special zombies barely spawn and don’t affect the game very much. The game on multiple occasions breaks the one thing that makes VR so different and interesting, immersion, it just looks cheap and feels cheap, I bought it on sale for 6 bucks and still feel like I wasted that money.
Poor graphics! Not a triple aaa title compared to my previous picks. I'm a graphics snob..I may go back to it but too many better games to play in the meantime
I’ve spent many hours playing the flatscreen version, and my friends and I had a blast. I honestly wouldn’t want to play it solo. Unfortunately, this version only offers single-player.

I originally wrote a nasty review about how dark the game is without maxed out settings changed & while I would still like to see a flashlight.. I have to say now I only want more levels!!! This is a killer zombie shooter!! I'm not really sweaty anymore & I get sick of games being ridiculously hard on easy setting.. Never could stand a boss fight & just can't compete online these days.. This game is perfect as easy is easy.. medium is medium & hard offers up some fairly intense action. I just can't stop playing & trying different approaches to getting thru the levels. Great job on this one guys & sorry about my original review.. 🙋‍♂️
Great zombie game ! Are there any future plans to add co-op ??

It’s a good game: solid graphics, attention to detail, and an excellent sense of massive zombie hordes (I honestly don’t know how they made this work on Quest).

Basically, there’s only one serious downside: the game gets repetitive pretty quickly. In practice, all you do is move between locations where you fight off waves of zombies. There’s no other gameplay.

I so glad I waited for the big sale went on at holidays I finally beaten the whole game great story missions last level took me minute figure out what I was suppose to do talking about crazy zombies wow definitely worth the sale price great work on the game devs ignore the haters who put one star and whining about the game

reviews.review_title: another great PS3 era shooter in VR
reviews.review_description:
what have World War Z and Bulletstorm in common besides literal bullet storms? Well, big engaging, larger than life action in epic locations and... absolute appalling reviews by users who clearly don't like videogames...

you don't see ports/adaptations like this every day on Quest, they're rare gems with graphics and action well beyond usual indie VR games. Enjoy rather than pay attention to fake complaints...

reviews.review_title: 5 star but some complaints abt this game
reviews.review_description:
I love the game, a combination of a fps shooter and a zombie game all in VR is great, I do have some complaints but it does not change the rating, for one, the game does not have enough lighting, you can still navigate but it's still pretty dark, my second complaint is the fact you have to find keys to unlock the turrets to ise them but over all, I'd recommend this it's great, it's an fps shooter and a zombie game all in VR ect. But do realize that it did come out a few months ago so it's obviously gonna have some sorta bug but, they'll probably fix it. P.s people say haptic feed back sucks but it is perfectly fine, and best part is you can use two guns at once  and choose a play style, like casual, or realistic or even custom, but I will say it's a little scary but still fun enough to play almost all missions but they do have some repetitive things but it's fine, and having hordes most of the time in the game is literally what the games about 
I hope this helps everyone who is thinking of buying the game

Great Game master shooting as many Zombies as you can  makes this Game challenging a great Game to  play and the  storyline Great also Recommend this Game  to keep in your  zombie Collection in your library to play again and again to master your skills  its Arizona Sunshine on Steroids .
Good fun, shame about no coop or multiplayer, a bit short, wish they would add coop, more maps and weapons, maybe even weapon customization

I don’t get the negative reviews. This game is well made and lots of fun. Is it perfect ? Of course not, but show me a game that is. The missing co-op is a bummer no doubt but the campaign is fun, the weapons feels great and overall it’s one of the very good games for VR.

reviews.review_title: I changed my mind after several tries.
reviews.review_description:
I am updating the review. 
So regardless my original review I gave it another go... Actually few more goes. And surprisingly the more I played the better it looked. I still will stand with idea that is just cheap copy of L4D, but is definitely playable. The problem is that in the start is just weird and not fun at all and would throw me away but I spend money and want to play enough to justify spending (I know I can refund easily but I tend to keep everything I buy even if it's not great game, in the end of the day the developers also want to eat and is not fair to play and then refund, is like ordering dish in restaurant and bite half of steak and say I don't like it and want full refund... at least I can't do it, I don't think is fair), but the more you go the more perks you open and better locations you explore. I still think it's not the best game out there, is lacking lots of features that can be implemented, like  for some users (like me) giving close to unlimited or even unlimited bulltes would be way better experience (just remove any achievement with that option), I like play to relax after work, I don't want another challenge 🤣 the feel when you shoot big round of bullets and to see muzzle flash is amazing (even is not how real gun behaves) 
Also you could have added some upgrades like laser sight etc (maybe it has but haven't found yet) 
So if you are zombie games lover you probably will enjoy this game, just don't expect too much and try to play for a bit before having a bad opinion. 


The old review:
I'm a big fan of zombie games and enjoy trying them all, so I was eager to check out this title. Unfortunately, it feels like a poorly executed VR imitation of Left 4 Dead. 
The experience is plagued by significantly worse graphics, clunky mechanics, and questionable mission design. A major issue is the gameplay loop: multiple waves attack from all directions, and you can run out of ammunition as early as the second wave. The gunplay lacks proper aiming (which might be a preference) and suffers from a surreal and excessive bullet spread, making accuracy nearly impossible. While ammo boxes exist, running dry mid-wave is common, and the waves themselves can nearly wipe out an AI teammate in one go. The AI teammates are also largely useless, often just standing stationary and shooting until the threat passes. Ultimately, despite being titled World War Z, it's just a worse version of the Left 4 Dead formula and, in my opinion, another simple cash grab. I found myself quickly returning to Arizona Sunshine. I genuinely wanted to enjoy it, and I'm glad I only purchased it at a steep discount, as the full price would have led to major disappointment.
Game feels like a offline only mode of a co op game i feel like I wasted my money and time im never trusting these devs or buying anything from them again they suck at making games

People who think the graphics are ugly are just bad, skill issue, the ai IS useful those guys just suck 👎🏻. The game is easy to beat but still fun i have beat every mission on the max difficulty and maxed everything except that one advanced smg

reviews.review_title: I love Achievenent Hunting this game!
reviews.review_description: It's dark, and the swarms of zombies are insane. I LOVE that, and keep wanting to jump back in. This is one game I actually don't use my open facial interface for, and it is exciting! Plus, it has Quest Achievements!
i love the game

It is almost a perfect game.
Set in the WWZ universe, you play as one of the operatives in different locations: urban setting in New York or in the streets of Tokio, with varied stages and objectives to accomplish in every run.
Hordes of zekes, but you have access to a varied arsenal to put them finally in rest . Pretty standard warfare in FPS games: hand guns, SMGs, shotguns and assault rifles to more high-powered weapons like grenade launchers and payload rifles (my favourite).
My only issue is the slightly unresponsive controls, otherwise I am very pleased with this fantastic game.

reviews.review_title: I can pay $50 if it supports multiplayer
reviews.review_description:
But without multiplayer, at this price, this is acceptable.

AI companions feel dumb, but (they are) better than nothing.

My only complaint is the lack of haptic feedback when grabbing something. I can't recognize whether I grabbed a gun or not during a hectic fight.

There are some unpolished points here and there, but I'm not sure they're gonna fix them.

Too many bugs, graphics are ugly, AI companions are useless, all while you move around unappealing locales to shoot waves of zombies. 

How you can take a multiplayer game and make it fully single player, thinking it will be successful, is puzzling. 

Don't believe the unending five star reviews, left by bots and paid accounts. This is not good and not worth the cost.
Very good game and the hold off type game play is fun but im waiting on multiplayer
Just a really ropey experience. Badly animated, ugly character models, janky reload animations and poor Ai. Not fun to play. I got this on deep sale but I'll try for a refund.
Super Game

Way cool lots of action npcs are great , game is well done. 👍 If u liked Arizona sunshine as much as I did 1 and 2 u will love this game has that vibe imho. PS please  Don't pay any attention to the whiny 1 and 2 stars rating butt hurt for co op goons here who only complaint about no multi-player cuzz they dont know how to play a game by themselves and need frs to help them. Game is fabulous as is with npcs and a must buy for all zombie shooting lovers. Btw I'm not a paid bot like some sore head goobers say here , I laugh at their dumb reviews of the game which are senseless babble just saying

reviews.review_title: Bots don’t move or help on Tokyo mission
reviews.review_description:
Bots don’t move or help
 on this mission

As a returning VR Veteran this game running Onboard is compelling in and of itself. But for 11 Missions and a handful of guns I can't say that I feel entirely great about this game.

As a Shooter and a Horde Shooter at that, the game is stellar, guns have good feedback and the Zombies respond accordingly in ways that wouldn't be outside the norm in Left For Dead 2.

However this is not Left For Dead 2.

The Bots dont heal themselves and never stop moving so you can heal them.

The Grenades are basically useless because you have to use both hands to use one, and you can only carry two, and your "Team" never ever trys even picking them up.

And the final and most Egregious Sin.

They Gatekeep Keys so thoroughly you'll rarely ever make it to the Cresendo of the level with enough Keys to put up a good defense.

Then when you do use them you find out the Sentry Turret is useless if more than 5 zombies are in its line of sight, the Mounted .50 doesn't have enough ammo and I think Spawns extra body-doubles when you shoot into Hordes to make it feel like it does more than it really is. And for some reason the crate of BARBED WIRE is more dangerous than a Machine Gun.

Pick your Poison. If you wanted Left For Dead 2 in VR this isnt it. If you wanted an Intense experience which will leave you screaming "WHAT DID I DO WRONG?!" on Easy for 3 hours only to find out that your AI feed themselves to the "Zeke" and dont heal automatically. But you stay with it because Holy hell was it enthralling and compelling the entire time by spectacle alone.

Remember to mist the vegetables, Keep all Firearms fully loaded at all times, and ALWAYS USE THE PORTABLE MACHINEGUN LAST!


EDIT: I've made it to Rank 10 and can further add to my Review.

The Final Mission is Flat-Out Broken and Unfinishable because the Lazer Designator is Broken and doesn't work  at all, to the point that it will actually break your ability to pick up any items at all, and it's cost me 3 Whole Runs of the Game.

I’m 42 years old and ￼￼decided to finally try my sons VR for the 1st time, Bought this game yesterday the moment it was released not having any experience with VR and I’m ￼addicted￼ to this game. You will not be disappointed! I throw the grenades under hand so I don’t ￼￼throw the remote even with it tied to my wrist￼ and I get great results, the guns are amazing with how you can ￼drop mag pull magazines from your ￼chest, reload ￼and chamber and it’s cool how you can put guns on ￼￼either side hip, your back and grenades on your chest. And you can hold some guns with a hand to have 3 at times! I was going to write a ￼review yesterday but was to lost in this game. If you’re only going to buy one game most likely like me, this is the one to buy!

reviews.review_title: Funny but short And no COOP mode
reviews.review_description:
Funny slaying horde zombie game with some well-known characters from PC version. 4 campaings. Good map design but very little repeatable potential. For one play Its Fun. Difficulty Is not much challenging. I play every mission on Hard And failed ONLY one pro two times. Shooting Feels great. Add attachments for weapons, melee weapons, COOP, more maps And i Will gladly play again. 
SCORE 3.9*

Hey!!    OMG I JUST HAD A BLAST ..   GREAT MOVEMENT. I SEE THEY TOOK TIME WITH THIS GAME .. I LOVE 💞 THE blasting AND HOW that shot gun  GUN sounds... OHH YAHHH..   LOVE THE GRAPHICS And INTRO.. IM FROM NEW YORK SO THAT INTRO IS CORRECT..  IM A MOVIE 🎥  IN SIDE OF HERE ... ONLY THING IT IS MISSING WITH THE HORDE OF ZOMBIES please put  MULTIPLAYER.. PLEASE GET IT IN STORY MODE ..guns are on point a real feeling when blasting ... Please put multiplayer.... I promise you won't go wrong

Guns feel good but at moment with A.I players as teammates you need make every bullet count 👍 

Secondly with A .I normal feels very hard and easy feels normal on a little more easier scale 😆 

Apart from this it's a good game with only room for improvement with each update to come 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 

I'm gradually enjoying it more with each playthrough with every upgrade to gameplay it's pulling me back in 

Please switch out these A.I players for real players

 🙏🤞 Add Matchmaking

reviews.review_title: Worth it but needs co op atleast
reviews.review_description: Pretty much a flawless game it could definitely be a MUST have for anyone if the devs add co-op and eventually more missions, doing those 2 things would easily put this in a top 5 must have for VR period! Seriously if you guys don't add co-op eventually it would be one of the biggest mistakes ever because you know just like I do 99% of people that have played any world war z game usually always played with friends... And the worthless people saying the good reviews are all bot comments are pathetic... Seriously some of these people act like just because they don't like something that it's automatically horrible, they definitely need more missions and multiplayer then bam really really great game

It's a really great game but I thought that there would be more updates.ive already unlocked everything and feel as tho there's nothing else I could do and since I had teammates that were bots I thought that eventually we would get multi-player but I was wrong.

reviews.review_title: Pretty good game, support it if you can
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: after playing hours of this game, im changing my review. People are pooping on it and in my opinion, this game is definitely deserving of your 20 dollars. To see the hordes 100's of zombies toppling over one another to get to you  is something im glad I got to experience in VR. There are a few issues (lack of multiplayer, few zombie skins, ect.) But I stand by what I say when I say to support this game.

*this game is 10x better if you play some heavy jams in the background*

reviews.review_title: So far it’s just as expected, THRILLING!
reviews.review_description: The only issue I’ve experienced so far is the grenades not tracking well and thrown very short even though I would chuck it in an arc as hard as I could. But besides that, enjoying the wide variety of weapons and the horror of being alone and mobbing down hordes of zombies !

Well I saw that 1-Star review and went in cautious.

Baring in mind there’s been a hefty patch since, I’ve personally had no trouble. Not sure what the AI issue is? They seem to kill zombies no differently from the console game. 

I do agree that it not being multiplayer is a downside because that’s what made it great, visuals just seem as similar as most VR games though sure I have seen better aswell. 

World War Z is about the large number of zombies and it delivers? Even if it feels like it could do more, but I really enjoyed it still for the price!

reviews.review_title: The only zombie game I'd recommend
reviews.review_description:
I'll be honest- I'm sick of zombie games on the Quest. Maybe you are too. So when I saw this game, I thought- *sigh* Another zombie game? Skip.

The promises did give me pause. World War Z style hordes in a VR game? On the Quest's hardware? For 20 dollars? Yeah right. It was almost comical to me. I bought this game, planning to refund it, just to see how bad it was.

I was pleasantly surprised! I'm a VR nut. I can't remember the last time I was eagerly awaiting my headset to recharge so I could hop back into a game. This is the first time in months I've felt a challenge in a shooter (on only medium difficulty no less). Visuals look pretty close to the trailer. Performance is solid. Any deaths I had were due to skill. The perks, unlocks, and end of level traps encourage replayability and experimentation. The ally AI is competent but doesn't break the difficulty.

Normally, I'd say skip all zombie games, including the beloved Arizona Sunshine series. But this one is a ton of fun and worth the money.
It was great. It kept me on my toes. I really enjoyed playing. I wish it had multiplayer tho

I really enjoyed this game. There are horde of zombies, and the action keeps you on your toes as you move around and gather supplies. It’s been a long time since I’ve played a game this intense and fun.

reviews.review_title: Best fun I've had in a while
reviews.review_description:
That rush of anxiety when you hear the rumblings of a giant horde about to descend upon your location. Fantastic! 

-Zero bugs (no idea what people are on about) 
-Graphics are pretty good (quest 3 with quest optimizer installed)
-Guns feel and sound absolutely solid. Satisfying kills. 
-Possibly a little short but still well worth the price. 

Would happily pay for DLC

Good job developers

reviews.review_title: This is what a Zombie game should be lik
reviews.review_description: WWZ quickly jumped into my top 5 - Zombie shooters of all time after playing it only once.  The hordes of zombies is amazing.  FAST, FAST zombies.  Zombies that blow up, zombies that spit blood to infect you, zombies that when killed spew out a green poison gas.  I can't wait for more locations to be added and also the ability to play with friends.

UDATE: Im gonna throw my fifth star up anyway because this game deserves way better ranking than it is currently receiving. Almost finished with this and I realize its the most fun Ive had with my quest in a long time, despite lack of co op.  Plus Devs already put out first patch so they arent slacking at all.       

ORIGINAL REVIEW: I was incredibly skeptical of this title , especially after seeing its made for Quest 2 and the co op was stripped from it, but DANG these are the best guns Ive seen in VR. Loud, punchy, and with the most robust muzzle flare Ive seen to date. Guns are amazing to shoot in this game!!! the flow of the levels and sheer amount of stampeding zombies very much reminded me of Left 4 Dead. This is a polished game with very little jank to complain about. I do however wish the location UI indicator wouldn't hover in our view, so def let us turn that off.  I will give 5th star for a Quest 3 visual upgrade or when co op is added.  If you like most of the big zombie games on the store , this is a must have

Solid 7/10 but needs improvements.
Pros:
Lots of guns and they are all fun to use
Dual wielding
Progression is done well and will keep the game going for a while
Tons of zombies to shoot!
Replayability 
Cons:
Graphic is just ok although it gets better in later levels.
Glitches here and there , nothing game breaking so far
No collectibles that could  extend the story apart from items used in the game 
AI companions need improvements .
Overall, I am pretty sure that anyone will enjoy it if they like zombie VR shooters.

This game is really fun, and quite good. I am loving all the action this game has to offer. Don’t let other VR players comments about it not being multiplayer keep you from owning this game. I’m sure it might be one day if not it’s still fun as ever by yourself. The guns feel great, the movement is great, there does seem to be a bug throwing grenades, but i’m sure it will be fixed. I think this game is better than any other zombie VR game. I backed out of the preorder thinking you would just kill zombies in a stationary position. I was wrong you get to move around. I am glad I made this awesome purchase after seeing all the reviews. Great job devs. 

Update : don’t listen to the cry babies over the graphics, or playability. If this is your style of game, and gameplay then get it. I personally like the graphics, and everything about this game. For the people that don’t understand VR games will get better with better standalone hardware. The quest 3 is what the devs had to work with. This is just as good as any other AAA VR game for standalone.

reviews.review_title: The Vr experience of the console game
reviews.review_description:
I have to say this game was pretty well made! 
controls are tight and love when that massive horde is coming gets my blood rushing

This is the game that Zombie Army VR wishes it were!
 I haven't had this much fun in a zombie shooter for a long long time. The guns are fantastic and feel really good to shoot, and the zombie hordes are an absolute blast to tear into with full auto. The grenades are easy to throw and require little effort to yeet them long distances, maybe a little bit too far? The explosions are a little underwhelming but with the amount of activity going on in the game during a horde rush, I can understand that compromises had to be made and I find it amazing that this runs so well on the Quest platform . The companions actually work well and at least on the lower difficulty are quite effective. They do make some dumb decisions but hey, it's a mobile platform, there's limits on the computation power available! 
All in all this is a real blast to play and I can see lots of replayability in this game. 
It would be great if one day an update would bring multiplayer because this would be so much fun to play with friends!

I noted that several other players like myself had problems with the preorder system and I hope that this can be resolved.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the support and for sharing your suggestions! 🙏 For the pre-order issue, we recommend that you get in touch with our support team and provide more details on it so that we can investigate: https://support.saber.games/hc/en/world-war-z-vr/articles/how-to-report-an-issue-6.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE THIS GAME SO MUCH!!!
reviews.review_description: I’ve been a fan of world war z for years I started playing the normal game when it came out and now they made this and I tried it out and my god 10 out of bloody 10 I love this game my new favourite vr game if you want o shoot zombies down please buy this game you will not be disappointed ❤️❤️❤️❤️

reviews.review_title: Game ending bug ill Success map Tokyo
reviews.review_description:
Game was great until I got into the third map
of Tokyo location. Bots dont move or shoot. If you manage to somehow make it to the end the mission will not end. Tried restarting mission multiple times. Plus somehow I keep randomly dropping my weapon every now and then.

developer_response.body: Hi, mdarwin1! Thank you for reporting these issues, and we're sorry that they affected your game progression. If possible, we recommend that you submit a ticket to our support team with some footage. They will investigate: https://support.saber.games/.

reviews.review_description:
It's a good game with cool graphics but the only problem so far with it is that you have to keep pressing the stick down for you to run in which that hurts people's thumb if they had to keep pressing over and over again. 
 please fix that problem in which it could would be some other options where you could run faster other than that it looks like it's a good game. But because of that one problem from me to keep pressing the stick down just to run, I am going to return the game back and get my refund and so that situation is fixed. Thank you

I had low expectations going in because of haters but I bought it anyway 🤷. And I am satisfied with my purchase. This game is more polished than 90% of games after they just come out. I can understand why people are upset about the no Multi-player aspect. I am too. But this game is baller son. 👌 I love games that overwhelm and make me feel almost helpless. You'll breeze through it on easy mode but normal and hard will have your trigger finger begging for mercy but you can't stop shooting 😂😂 I'm a semi or burst shooter to conserve ammo but found myself hoplessy spraying alot in this game. I Havnt experienced any bugs yet but it's only been a few sessions. Ai can be a bit dumb but definitely not useless. Some things I would like to see in future updates would be some kind of weapon attachment system. Flashlights, lazers, grips things like that. Multi-player of course maybe a little more variety in zombies but I mean with that many coming at you who cares what they look like 😂 big thanks to the devs for this one. And I look forward to seeing what this game becomes over time.

The game is just pure fun,
Yes they could have put coop mode,
Yes it's not perfect,
But mowing hundreds of zombies with a machine gun never gets old despite what everyone tends to repeat (another vr zombie game),
It runs surprisingly well on the quest 3.

I hope this game does well and they add coop plus more levels, game mode etc...

I was hoping this game would be good. I have the PS 5 version so I wanted something like it for my VR. But the game is too buggy. I can’t make it passed the last mission in in France because once I’m able to save the helicopter, my character stops grabbing things. I can’t grab my weapons to defend myself and I certainly can’t grab the rope to escape. So i essentially can’t beat the mission and just end up dying. I’m gonna need my money back

Keeping it short. I'm enjoying the game so far. Gotta love the amount of zombies you have to take on. The graphics are pretty good and the sound design is immersive. The game just released and to my surprise it plays pretty well. Just needs Co-op and Hoard mode.

I played the crap outta the console version and my honest thoughts are the VR title is a great time does it need work adjusting some things like the audio lighting and gameplay yeah 

the A.I. companions do need some better pathing and whenever a hoard does appear for them to pull out their main weapons right away instead of using their sidearms and some of the guns do need adjustments for their damage and recoil and a headshot should be a kill in my opinion fully unloading the pistol when trying to stay quiet at least for the screamers and gas bags 

I would like to see some more customization options either some more gun skins or character skins but at least being able to put a red dot sight on the guns so when your duel welding it's easier to land shots 

And hopefully new levels or weapons can be added and maybe a melee option like the console version 

But overall I'm having a blast with it!

To me I like the sound design, I like how the weapons shoot, I like how the zombies run at you and rushes at you to me no other zombie games do that properly on Quest. The bad Hit Detection sucks the zombies don’t even really hit you and it takes health, Weapon Ammo isn’t nearly enough for the final sections when they all rush at you, while the maps look great to me the design is more of a go down this street, shoot waves of zombies, and then enter building and defend X but to me that’s most zombie games issues on Quest except RE4 and Walking Dead games

reviews.review_title: Korean language support is urgently need
reviews.review_description:
The game was more playable than I expected. 

You might be disappointed if you're expecting something like the Arizona Sunshine series. But if you want to have fun shooting guns, you'll be satisfied.

However, if that's the case, I recommend switching to easy reload mode. 

The game itself is linear, but it's not bad.
It keeps you focused.

By the way, I wish they would support Korean.  
Our Korean friends are waiting for that.

Ok devs I'm going to give yah an honest review the game is really really good my favorite zombie shooter right now but all I'm asking is don't abandon this game give us updates fixes enhance the graphics and lighting for the quest 3 I know yah can do it and work a bit more on the performance give us more maps missions and please check the weapon reloading system make it a bit smoother the game is really great and I recommend to anyone as long as yah don't abandon the game and listen to the players feedback one more thing please add the Spanish audio

Really enjoying this, I think it's awesome. Graphics are incredible,  really smooth and detailed. Gameplay is frantic, but amazing fun. I used to play the flatscreen version, and this captures the gameplay beautifully. Multi-player would make this game legendary. The number of zombies on screen is like nothing else on VR. Well done to the team behind this👍

I absolutely love it,  it absolutely feels like wwz. Guns have some real punch👊🏽
I always wondered what it would feel like in vr wwz. I even play in first person on the flat. This is almost perfect.  More episodes please and thank you

Mainly regarding the fact that there's no flashlight in the game, and sometimes the spike in difficulty between the settings is a bit much. I have fun, but I'm also a zombie addict, so I am biased.

For what it is, the game looks very good, as I wouldn't expect too much from a game that's only being charged $20 for. The AI is adequate for killing the zombies, but they cannot carry anything, or use health kits themselves.

I really love this game—I’ve played over 500 hours on PC. The VR version performs quite well overall, but there are still several areas that could be improved:
	1.	I hope the weapon system can be more like the PC version, where upgrades come with additional attachments, not just different skins.
	2.	I’d like to see more chapters added in the future, even if they are paid expansions.
	3.	I hope the game will support the Chinese language.
	4.	It would be great if pistols could also have melee attack functions.
5.more guns ！

I used to play world war z on  my pc  but since vr I not play much flat gaming  on pc. So I was glad to hear it was coming to vr  and I got to say it's a great game I feel like I'm there in the middle of it. All  I love it It would be  great if you could have multiplayer and some more levels but  loved this game come on developers you did  great now lets do  greater  with an update

This definitely is what I thought it would turn out to be.
It’s very bland and without co op it gets boring very fast.
It can be fun…
But that fun doesn’t last long because it’s repetitive ..
Oh well, could have been awesome

I beat the whole game in 1 night.

Payed $20 for a single night.

I expected a little more content but whatever. I'd play again.

I ask you kindly, could you add a horde mode to it like the others?

I feel that would spark the match on the gasoline if you catch my meaning. 😈✨️

Seriously this game should not be $20 it should be $10 or $15 at least. It was very short and easy to beat on all modes. It was immensely underwhelming for the franchise.

I feel like I was taking a stroll through the entire game, wait... I was because there was no real challenges.

This first release exceeds my expectations. The action each mission is so thrilling, it makes me come back for more.

If Saber is going to extend with multiplayer it’s going to be a true classic, but even the single player is awesome. Thanks Saber WWZ VR team for your efforts !

Awesome game, its similar to After the Fall but single player only, which is ok for me since I prefer single player games.

 You get two AI controlled bots and they can die and you can heal them too. Their AI is decent. 
Graphics are high definition and smooth, everything looks and feels polished.

Levels look varied and fun to explore, there are zombies everywhere but a real challenge are those set pieces where you have to defend civilians etc from massive waves of zombies. You find special keys which can be used for automated turrets, manual turrets, traps, etc. There is some variety and its very fun (and challenging) overall.

There are different zombie types, from cannon fodder to more tanky ones, explosing ones, reinforcement calling zombies etc,

There are also some checkpoints during the mission so if you fail a set piece you can always reload from a checkpoint and try again. 

There is also a skill tree and multiple characters for flavour.

Great game overall and well worth its price.
You will be mowing down hundreds of Zeeks at a time!

I dont get the negative reviews. Buggy? No. Ugly graphics? They're fine. The gameplay is pretty simple. You fight your way to a checkpoint, then , you fight a huge hoard. Shooting the zombies is satisfying, especially when you blow up a hoard thats climbing a wall. The levels are varied enough to not feel repetitive, and some actually look nice.

This game isn’t bad at all — far from it — but it could be much better. The graphics are quite good for a Quest title, and the environments don’t pale in comparison to Arizona Sunshine 2. Sure, the zombies aren’t as well made as in Arizona Sunshine 2, but there are far more of them, and the environments are bigger and more open.

The gunplay could feel better, but it’s fine overall. However, zombies always tend to keep their distance, which feels odd. Climbing ladders feels like being stuck on rails. Performance can sometimes be shaky, even in closed areas without too many zombies, though this should improve with future updates (it’s already better with the latest patch).

The two main flaws are:

The lack of multiplayer. Even the first Arizona Sunshine had it! I really hope it will be added later. The developers actually shot themselves in the foot here, since this decision immediately hurt the game’s reputation.

The low-budget feel. NPCs don’t move, their lips don’t sync when they talk, etc. This really needs improvement.


For €20, it’s a good deal, but I would rather have paid €40 for a more polished game, especially in terms of staging and presentation. Even with a small budget, you should never overlook that. In the end, good presentation matters more than graphics.

Thanks to the developers for this game — I really hope you’ll keep improving it and add a co-op mode. Even if it’s just for two players, that would already be great.

I, like many others probably, am very disappointed that multiplayer has been removed from a title originally built for multiplayer. At least a co-op mode could have been included. This is so disappointing because the game is crying out for one throughout. It could have easily placed it alongside Arizona Sunshine, Death Horizon, or the recently released Zombie Army, all of which greatly benefit from co-op. And even though World War Z can't compete with those titles in terms of graphics or length, it's still a lot of fun mowing down the massive zombie hordes. But it could have been terrific fun for two players – missed opportunity.

I want to kill ALOT of zombies 

And this game delivers and also the people complaining about the graphics... brother it's a quest 3 game about killing again ALOT of zombies .... a lot of zombies  on screen at once sorry if you were expecting ultra 50k ray tracing ...
Just be happy and kill alot of zombies

Game looks good and  runs flawless. Sound is good.  Exiting game. Big guns.
A good mix of relaxed gameplay and intense showdowns.
 i haven't experienced any glitches playing on quest 3s.
People giving this 1 star is ridiculous.
Just finished it on hard mode. Amazing game.
More maps please.

Really getting into this game now, the graphics are decent and the storyline and unlockables are good to keep you coming back: 

Multiplayer would be amazing in this game as the companions are not that great ha ha 

But like I say I’m enjoying it great game please make it multiplayer

this game is what left 4 dead could be if it had official vr support 


i did encounter some bugs such as
ghost weapons on teammates after they respawn,
unable to heal teammates,
The healthbar above teammates don't show,
and sometimes stand around like idiots.


however,i did still have a ton of fun,my only real complaint is it was pretty short for a wwz game.

reviews.review_title: It's good but needs some work
reviews.review_description:
This game is pretty good but it does needs a lot of work to be great.
I used to play allot on pc the desktop version and this game is not a port of that game so it is a separate stand alone in the WWZ universe.
Graphics are ok for running local on the Quest and with the Game Optimiser you can tweak the resolution for sharper visuals.
The most problems are bugs in terms of grabbing objects and navigating.
If they polish this game like the Arizona games it would be much better and I will give 4 stars.
Also reloading shotguns is a bit clunky.
Some animations from the zombies could use some work.
I don't write reviews but i do for this one for the devs maybe take notes and work on this game to polish things up.

Nice graphics for a Quest game, an a lot of fun and action.

One of the few games I actually enjoyed. finishing.

Some minor bugs like the granades do not reliably lose the pin when pulling with teeth before throwing.

I did purchase the game, btw.
